# nxos_analytics (Resource)

This resource can manage the Analytics configuration on NX-OS devices, including instances, profiles, events, policies, and traffic analytics.

### API Documentation

- [analyticsEntity](https://pubhub.devnetcloud.com/media/dme-docs-10-5-3/docs/System/analytics:Entity/)
- [analyticsInst](https://pubhub.devnetcloud.com/media/dme-docs-10-5-3/docs/System/analytics:Inst/)
- [analyticsProfile](https://pubhub.devnetcloud.com/media/dme-docs-10-5-3/docs/System/analytics:Profile/)
- [analyticsEvents](https://pubhub.devnetcloud.com/media/dme-docs-10-5-3/docs/System/analytics:Events/)
- [analyticsPolicy](https://pubhub.devnetcloud.com/media/dme-docs-10-5-3/docs/System/analytics:Policy/)
- [analyticsTrafficAnalytics](https://pubhub.devnetcloud.com/media/dme-docs-10-5-3/docs/System/analytics:TrafficAnalytics/)

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "nxos_analytics" "example" {
  instances = {
    "analytics" = {
      admin_state              = "enabled"
      collect_tunnel_header    = true
      control                  = "stateful-ha"
      enable_analytics_submode = true
      geneve_enable            = true
      timeout                  = 30
      profiles = {
        "PROFILE1" = {
          burst_interval_shift                 = 10
          collect_interval                     = 200
          description                          = "My profile"
          ip_packet_id_shift                   = 10
          mtu                                  = 1500
          sequence_number_guess_threshold_high = 100
          sequence_number_guess_threshold_low  = 50
          source_port                          = 10000
        }
      }
      events = {
        "EVENTS1" = {
          acl_drops              = true
          black_hole             = true
          buffer_drops           = true
          description            = "My events"
          event_export_max       = 10
          forward_drops          = true
          group_drop_events      = true
          group_latency_events   = true
          group_packet_events    = true
          ip_dont_fragment       = true
          latency_threshold      = 100
          latency_threshold_unit = "milli-sec"
          receive_window_zero    = true
          tos                    = 10
          tos_enable             = true
          ttl_match_enable       = true
          ttl_match_value        = 10
        }
      }
      policies = {
        "POLICY1" = {
          description = "My policy"
        }
      }
      traffic_analytics_description                  = "My traffic analytics"
      traffic_analytics_interface_mode               = true
      traffic_analytics_name                         = "TA1"
      traffic_analytics_service_database_size        = 1000
      traffic_analytics_troubleshoot_export_interval = 30
      traffic_analytics_udp_port_list                = "1234"
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `device` (String) A device name from the provider configuration.
- `instances` (Attributes Map) Holds the Analytics instance information.
  - Map key: `mode` - Mode Select to represent the feature.
  - Key choices: `analytics`, `netflow` (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--instances))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The distinguished name of the object.

<a id="nestedatt--instances"></a>
### Nested Schema for `instances`

Optional:

- `admin_state` (String) The administrative state of the object or policy.
  - Choices: `enabled`, `disabled`
- `collect_tunnel_header` (Boolean) Enable collection of tunnel header data on QP_AR.
- `control` (String) The control state.
  - Choices: `stateful-ha`
- `enable_analytics_submode` (Boolean) Enable Analytics Submode funxtionality.
- `events` (Attributes Map) Configure FTE Events Information.
  - Map key: `name` - Object name. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--instances--events))
- `geneve_enable` (Boolean) Enable parsing of Geneve header mentioned in RFC8926.
- `policies` (Attributes Map) Policy.
  - Map key: `name` - Object name. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--instances--policies))
- `profiles` (Attributes Map) Profile.
  - Map key: `name` - Object name. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--instances--profiles))
- `timeout` (Number) Flow timeout in seconds.
  - Range: `5`-`60`
- `traffic_analytics_description` (String) Description of the specified attribute.
- `traffic_analytics_interface_mode` (Boolean) Enable interface traffic analytics.
- `traffic_analytics_name` (String) Object name.
- `traffic_analytics_service_database_size` (Number) Maximum number of flows.
  - Range: `1`-`5000`
- `traffic_analytics_troubleshoot_export_interval` (Number) Troubleshoot flows export interval.
  - Range: `10`-`60`
- `traffic_analytics_udp_port_list` (String) UDP Port List.

<a id="nestedatt--instances--events"></a>
### Nested Schema for `instances.events`

Optional:

- `acl_drops` (Boolean) Configure the capture acl-drops in fte-event-drop-events mode for FTE Event.
- `black_hole` (Boolean) Configure the capture blackhole in fte-event-drop-events mode for FTE Record.
- `buffer_drops` (Boolean) Configure the capture buffer-drops in fte-event-drop-events mode for FTE Record.
- `description` (String) Description of the specified attribute.
- `event_export_max` (Number) Configure FTE Event Export Max Value.
  - Range: `0`-`32000`
- `forward_drops` (Boolean) Configure the capture forward-drops in fte-event-drop-events mode for FTE Record.
- `group_drop_events` (Boolean) Configure the group drop events for FTE Event.
- `group_latency_events` (Boolean) Configure the group latency events for FTE Event.
- `group_packet_events` (Boolean) Configure the group packet events for FT Event.
- `ip_dont_fragment` (Boolean) Configuration to capture events with IP: Don't Fragment flag.
- `latency_threshold` (Number) Configure FTE Latency Threshold limit.
  - Range: `0`-`65535`
- `latency_threshold_unit` (String) Configure FTE Latency Threshold Unit.
  - Choices: `micro-sec`, `milli-sec`
- `receive_window_zero` (Boolean) Configuration to capture events for TCP receive window zero.
- `tos` (Number) Configuration to capture events with given IP DSCP values.
  - Range: `0`-`255`
- `tos_enable` (Boolean) Configuration to enable events with given IP DSCP values.
- `ttl_match_enable` (Boolean) Configuration to enable ttl match events.
- `ttl_match_value` (Number) Configuration to capture events for given ttl value.
  - Range: `0`-`255`


<a id="nestedatt--instances--policies"></a>
### Nested Schema for `instances.policies`

Optional:

- `description` (String) Description of the specified attribute.


<a id="nestedatt--instances--profiles"></a>
### Nested Schema for `instances.profiles`

Optional:

- `burst_interval_shift` (Number) Timestamp Shift Value for Burst Interval.
  - Range: `0`-`255`
- `collect_interval` (Number) Collect Interval in Milliseconds.
  - Range: `100`-`64000`
- `description` (String) Description of the specified attribute.
- `ip_packet_id_shift` (Number) Analytics IP Packet Id Shift.
  - Range: `0`-`255`
- `mtu` (Number) MTU for the Analytics flow record packets.
  - Range: `576`-`9216`
- `sequence_number_guess_threshold_high` (Number) Sequence Number Guess Threshold High.
  - Range: `0`-`4294967295`
- `sequence_number_guess_threshold_low` (Number) Sequence Number Guess Threshold Low.
  - Range: `0`-`4294967295`
- `source_port` (Number) Source L4 Port for the Analytics packets.
  - Range: `1`-`65535`

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `identity` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to       = nxos_analytics.example
  identity = {}
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
### Identity Schema


#### Optional

- `device` (String) A device name from the provider configuration.

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = nxos_analytics.example
  id = ""
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import nxos_analytics.example ""
```
